title: Roku Q4 2023 Earnings Summary - Deadline
text: Roku surpassed Wall Street expectations in its fourth-quarter earnings report, released February 15, 2024. The streaming platform reported revenue of $984.4 million, exceeding analyst estimates of $964.2 million. The company achieved a milestone by reaching 80 million active accounts globally, up from 70 million the previous year. Additionally, 2023 marked the first year Roku exceeded 100 billion streaming hours. The net loss per share improved to 55 cents, compared to $1.70 in the year-ago period. However, average revenue per user declined 4% year-over-year to $39.92, attributed to growth in non-U.S. markets where monetization remains lower. Despite beating revenue forecasts, investors reacted negatively to the company's outlook. Roku shares fell 14% in after-hours trading following disclosure that advertising recovery has been "uneven." Management highlighted a significant industry shift: as streaming services introduce ad-supported subscription options, "consumers will seek savings via ad-supported subscriptions." Roku positioned itself advantageously to capitalize on advertising migration from traditional television to streaming platforms. The company projects first-quarter revenue of $850 million.
